fields.value: *Changelings are NOT an exception to, nor a way to work around, character slot limitations. Attempting to make a Changeling profile with multiple references/personas that should be unique characters will NOT be approved. When submitting a Changeling, your profile is allowed a MAXIMUM of the base unchanged form, and ONE changed form to be represented in the profile (multiple references for the form IS allowed). For forms beyond that (physical appearance, personality, or both), you will be required to dedicate an additional character slot per additional form you add to the profile. Backgrounds which grant an additional persona as their feature may include one additional one without an additional slot.*

fields.name: тЪая╕П Note
fields.value:
*Shifters are NOT werewolves. While Shifters may be descended from were-beasts, they are not fully lycanthropic. Their shifted forms draw on bestial traits, but are not a full animal-hybrid. Avoid visuals that clearly depict fully-transformed hybrid werewolves. When choosing reference art, aim for something that reflects a humanoid with subtle or partial bestial featuresтАФlike enhanced eyes, fangs, claws, fur accents, or other traits that suggest a primal heritage, not a complete hybrid-transformation. A shifter profile with a werewolf or animal-hybrid reference will not be approved (barring another source of transformation, such as a class feature or spell).
Hybrid transformations are permitted through the use of Lycan Blood Hunter or Beast Barbarian. Alternately, a fully humanoid canine (with no shifting capabilities) may be achieved through Custom Lineage, and werewolf characters (with non-transferrable lycanthropic curse) can be achieved through the use of NPC slots.*

embeds.title: Custom Origin
embeds.description:
`                                                                     `
Tasha's Cauldron of Everything introduced an optional rule to customize aspects of your character. The default ability score increases / languages / and proficiencies for each race reflects the typical members of that race, but not all individuals are the same. You may customize your character in the following ways:
**If you choose any of these options, you must specify the changes when presenting your character for approval.**

fields.name: Ability Score Increases
fields.value:
You may choose to ignore your standard racial ASI and assign ability score increases tailored to your character by taking any ability score increase you gain in your race or subrace and apply it to an ability score of your choice.
 тАв You can't apply multiple increases to the same ability score.
 тАв You canтАЩt increase a score above 20.

*This is built into Beyond, and [our custom GSheet](https://bit.ly/dol-gsheet).*

fields.name: Languages
fields.value: You may replace any language granted to you with another language from the following list: Abyssal, Celestial, Common, Deep Speech, Draconic, Dwarvish, Elvish, Giant, Gnomish, Goblin, Halfling, Infernal, Orc, Primordial, Sylvan, or Undercommon.

fields.name: Proficiencies
fields.value:
You can replace any proficiency granted by your race with a different one of your choice, following the table below.
```cs
[Granted Proficiency      Replacement Proficiency Options]
 Armor                    Simple/Martial Weapon or Tool
 Martial Weapon           Simple/Martial Weapon or Tool
 Simple Weapon            Simple Weapon or Tool
 Tool                     Tool or Simple Weapon```

fields.name: Changing a Skill
fields.value: Sometimes you pick a skill proficiency that ends up not being very useful in the campaign or that no longer fits your characterтАЩs story. In those cases, talk to your DM about replacing that skill proficiency with another skill proficiency offered by your class at 1st level. A convenient time for such a change is when you reach a level that grants you the Ability Score Increase feature, representing that your character has spent a level or two studying the new skill and letting the old one atrophy.

fields.name: Changing Your Subclass
fields.value:
Each character class involves the choice of a subclass at 1st, 2nd, or 3rd level. A subclass represents an area of specialization and offers different class features as you level up. You can change your subclass only when you gain a level that would normally give a new subclass feature.
If you decide to make this change, choose another subclass that belongs to your class and replace all your old subclass features with the features of the new subclass that are for your new level and lower.

fields.name: Changing Your Spells
fields.value:
In order to allow players the flexibility to choose spells for the purposes of (e)RP, while not locking them into potentially sub-optimal choices for the purposes of duels and tables, we allow the UA Spell Versatility feature as described in [this PDF](https://media.wizards.com/2019/dnd/downloads/UA-ClassFeatures.pdf):
In short, this feature allows non-preparation casters (Bards / Rangers / Sorcerers / Warlocks) to swap spells once per long rest, as follows:
```Whenever you finish a long rest, you can replace one spell you learned from your Spellcasting feature with another spell from your spell list. The new spell must be the same level as the spell you replace.```
